name: PR Review
description: Transcribed as data (#1284 Packet 1) from build_review_graph (crates/darkmux-lab/src/lab/review.rs), the Task/Step graph `darkmux mission launch review` builds (retired from `darkmux pr-review run` in Packet 4b): investigate (resolve bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat, dedup the flags) -> adjudicate (double-confirm judge over the deduped flags) -> report (verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts). As of Packet 3, this document is the EXECUTABLE source of truth -- build_review_graph is a thin launcher that loads this config, resolves crew_staffing into the probe-template task's `expand` collection, and calls mission_config::interpret. `src/mission_launch_review.rs` is the operator-facing CALLER of that launcher (`--param key=value` inputs below), not a second graph builder.

inputs.name: diff_file
inputs.description: Path to the unified diff file under review. `mission_launch_review::launch` reads it into ReviewStepContext.diff and feeds it to every probe/judge/verify dispatch. Required unconditionally -- even a `from_envelope` synthesis-only launch needs it, to resolve each finding's quoted anchor back to a diff line. Old flag: `--diff`.

inputs.name: case_id
inputs.description: Unique id for this review run (ReviewStepContext.case_id) -- correlates flow records and the review envelope, and seeds the minted mission's instance id. LAUNCHER-DERIVED when absent: `owner/repo@head_sha` for a GitHub source, the worktree path, or `local` -- an explicit value always wins. Never asked of the operator by the generic missing-inputs gate (mirrors `coder-phase`'s `mission_id` input).

inputs.name: worktree
inputs.description: Local checkout directory to bundle from. Alternative to `github` + `head_sha` (mutually exclusive; exactly one source is required unless `from_envelope`). Old flag: `--worktree`.

inputs.name: github
inputs.description: GitHub source `owner/repo` -- bundles via the GitHub API, no local checkout. Requires `head_sha`. Old flag: `--github`.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: Commit SHA to read file content at (GitHub source only). Old flag: `--head-sha`.

inputs.name: intent_file
inputs.description: Path to a file carrying the author's stated intent for the change (PR title + description, one blob), fed into the JUDGE prompt only (ReviewStepContext.intent_body; intent_title stays empty -- matches the retired CLI). Absent -> "(no description provided)". Old flag: `--intent-file`.

inputs.name: crew
inputs.description: Crew name from profiles.json's "crews" map, staffing the review-probe/review-judge/review-verify seats. Required unless `from_envelope` (synthesis-only needs no crew). Old flag: `--crew`.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Model-cycling mode: sequential | parallel | auto (default auto). Old flag: `--mode`.

inputs.name: k
inputs.description: Overrides every review-probe staffing's draw count (k). Old flag: `--k`.

inputs.name: envelope_out
inputs.description: Path to write the full review envelope (pretty JSON). Old flag: `--envelope-out`.

inputs.name: emit
inputs.description: Path to write the rendered `{mode, review, comment}` payload. `-` (or omitted) writes to stdout -- a CONTRACT the CI workflow parses byte-for-byte, so `mission launch` prints no decorative banner ahead of it for this config. Old flag: `--emit`.

inputs.name: profiles
inputs.description: Override path for profiles.json (else DARKMUX_PROFILES / the default resolution). Old flag: `--profiles-file`.

inputs.name: attribution
inputs.description: Attribution text for the posted footer (#1113) -- the default derives "where the model ran" from the envelope's own member records. Old flag: `--attribution`.

inputs.name: bundler
inputs.description: External bundler command (`<cmd> --worktree <dir> --diff <file>`), standing in for the built-in bundler. Old flag: `--bundler`.

inputs.name: charges_file
inputs.description: Re-judge a previously-recorded flag list without re-running the probe (run_judge_only) -- the bundler still runs, since the judge needs the code each flag's bundle_id refers to. Mints NO mission (same as the retired CLI). Old flag: `--charges-file`.

inputs.name: from_envelope
inputs.description: Synthesis-only: read a previously-written envelope_out and emit the rendered payload -- zero model calls, zero bundling, zero mission minted. The CI-testable path. Old flag: `--from-envelope`.

inputs.name: intent_title
inputs.description: INTERNALLY DERIVED, never operator-supplied -- `mission_launch_review::run_dispatch` always passes an empty string (matches the retired CLI's own RunOpts shape, which carried one intent blob with no separate title field). ReviewStepContext.intent_title.

inputs.name: crew_staffing
inputs.description: Resolved probe/judge/verify seat staffing (CrewStaffingSnapshot / ResolvedSeatStaffing), computed by `mission_launch_review::run_dispatch` from the resolved `crew` input -- never a literal CLI input. Determines HOW MANY review.probe:<seat-name> tasks the investigate phase's probe-template task expands into at launch time -- see the 'review-probe-template' task's notes below.

inputs.name: judge_concurrency
inputs.description: The judge step's real config.concurrency, resolved by the launcher from darkmux_types::config_access::review_judge_concurrency() (env > config.review.judge_concurrency > 1) -- never a literal CLI input. ALWAYS supplied by the launcher as a step_config_override on review-judge-step -- the static `config.concurrency: 1` below is a documented DEFAULT for a human reading this file, never load-bearing at launch.

phases.description: resolve review bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat, dedup

tasks.description: resolve review bundles from the diff

TEMPLATE for the probe stage. build_review_graph builds ONE task/step PER STAFFED PROBE SEAT (kind `review.probe:<seat-name>`, ids `review-probe-{index}-task` / `review-probe-{index}-step`) -- a count and a set of names only known once `crew_staffing` (see inputs) resolves. The `expand` block below (#1284 Packet 3, schema 1.1) is the typed, interpretable primitive mission_config::interpret uses to turn this ONE template into N real tasks at launch -- the launcher resolves `probe_seats` from crew_staffing's staffed probe seats (in staffing order) and passes it as a LaunchParams.expansions entry.
dedup probe flags — mechanism-family keying + anchor matching
double-confirm judge — internal pass1/pass2 loop over deduped flags

phases.description: verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts

notes: always present in the graph even when no verify seat is staffed -- ReviewVerifyStepKind itself no-ops on an absent seat; this config does not (yet) express that conditionality.

tasks.description: synthesis — finalize tier counts + needs-check clustering
